message:
Add git worktree tests and expand CLI/tools test coverage

- worktree_test.go: New tests for IsGitRepository, WorktreeManager existence
  checks, and worktree list parsing
- cleanup_test.go, ensemble_estimate_test.go, get_all_session_text_test.go:
  Expand test coverage with additional scenarios and edge cases
- adapter_test.go: Add 70+ lines testing tool adapter configuration and
  invocation patterns
- Sync beads issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1t881","title":"Test: Unit tests for LimitDetector pattern matching","description":"## Purpose
Comprehensive unit tests for LimitDetector pattern matching against actual agent output, covering all rate limit variations.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/limit_detector_test.go
package swarm

import (
    "testing"
    "time"
)

func TestLimitDetector_PatternMatching(t *testing.T) {
    detector := NewLimitDetector(testLogger(t))
    
    // Test all known rate limit patterns
    patterns := []struct {
        name       string
        agentType  string
        output     string
        shouldMatch bool
        matchType   string
    }{
        // Claude Code patterns
        {
            name:       "claude_rate_limit_standard",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "Error: rate limit exceeded. Please wait before trying again.",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "claude_429_error",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "API Error 429: Too Many Requests",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "429",
        },
        {
            name:       "claude_token_limit",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "You have exceeded your token limit for this billing period",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "token_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "claude_normal_output",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "I'll help you implement that function. Let me start by...",
            shouldMatch: false,
        },
        
        // Codex patterns
        {
            name:       "codex_rate_limit",
            agentType:  "cod",
            output:     "openai.RateLimitError: Rate limit reached for default-gpt-4",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "codex_quota_exceeded",
            agentType:  "cod",
            output:     "You exceeded your current quota, please check your plan",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "quota",
        },
        
        // Gemini patterns  
        {
            name:       "gemini_resource_exhausted",
            agentType:  "gmi",
            output:     "google.api_core.exceptions.ResourceExhausted: 429 Resource exhausted",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "resource_exhausted",
        },
        {
            name:       "gemini_quota_limit",
            agentType:  "gmi",
            output:     "Quota exceeded for GenerateContent",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "quota",
        },
        
        // Edge cases
        {
            name:       "partial_match",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "The word rate appears but limit does not follow immediately",
            shouldMatch: false,
        },
        {
            name:       "case_insensitive",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "RATE LIMIT EXCEEDED",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "multiline_output",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "Processing...\nWorking...\nError: rate limit exceeded\nRetrying...",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "empty_output",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "",
            shouldMatch: false,
        },
    }
    
    for _, tt := range patterns {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Agent: %s", tt.agentType)
            t.Logf("Output: %q", truncateForLog(tt.output, 100))
            t.Logf("Expected match: %v", tt.shouldMatch)
            
            match, pattern := detector.CheckOutput(tt.agentType, tt.output)
            
            t.Logf("Result: match=%v, pattern=%q", match, pattern)
            
            if tt.shouldMatch {
                assert.True(t, match, "Should detect rate limit")
                assert.NotEmpty(t, pattern, "Should identify pattern type")
                if tt.matchType != "" {
                    assert.Contains(t, pattern, tt.matchType, "Pattern type mismatch")
                }
            } else {
                assert.False(t, match, "Should NOT detect rate limit")
            }
        })
    }
}

func TestLimitDetector_CapturePane(t *testing.T) {
    // Create mock tmux client
    mockTmux := &MockTmuxClient{
        captureResponses: map[string]string{
            "cc_agents_1:1.0": "Normal agent output here",
            "cc_agents_1:1.1": "Error: rate limit exceeded",
            "cc_agents_1:1.2": "", // Empty pane
        },
    }
    
    detector := NewLimitDetector(testLogger(t))
    detector.tmux = mockTmux
    
    tests := []struct {
        paneID      string
        expectLimit bool
    }{
        {"cc_agents_1:1.0", false},
        {"cc_agents_1:1.1", true},
        {"cc_agents_1:1.2", false},
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.paneID, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Capturing pane: %s", tt.paneID)
            
            output, err := detector.CaptureAndCheck(tt.paneID, "cc")
            require.NoError(t, err)
            
            t.Logf("Captured output length: %d", len(output))
            t.Logf("Limit detected: %v", tt.expectLimit)
            
            match, _ := detector.CheckOutput("cc", output)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.expectLimit, match)
        })
    }
}

func TestLimitDetector_BackgroundMonitoring(t *testing.T) {
    mockTmux := &MockTmuxClient{}
    detector := NewLimitDetector(testLogger(t))
    detector.tmux = mockTmux
    detector.pollInterval = 50 * time.Millisecond
    
    t.Log("Starting background monitoring")
    
    ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 200*time.Millisecond)
    defer cancel()
    
    eventCh := detector.Events()
    
    // Start monitoring
    go detector.StartMonitoring(ctx, []PaneSpec{
        {Index: 1, AgentType: "cc"},
    }, "test_session")
    
    // Inject rate limit after short delay
    go func() {
        time.Sleep(75 * time.Millisecond)
        mockTmux.SetCaptureResponse("test_session:1.1", "rate limit exceeded")
    }()
    
    // Wait for event
    select {
    case event := <-eventCh:
        t.Logf("Event received: %+v", event)
        assert.Equal(t, "cc", event.AgentType)
        assert.Contains(t, event.Pattern, "rate")
    case <-ctx.Done():
        t.Log("Context cancelled before event (expected if timing varies)")
    }
}

// Helpers
func truncateForLog(s string, max int) string {
    if len(s) <= max {
        return s
    }
    return s[:max] + "..."
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log each test case input (agent type, output snippet)
- Log expected vs actual match results
- Log pattern type when matched
- Log pane capture details
- Log background monitoring events

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test all known Claude Code limit patterns
- [ ] Test all known Codex limit patterns
- [ ] Test all known Gemini limit patterns
- [ ] Test case insensitivity
- [ ] Test multiline output
- [ ] Test empty output
- [ ] Test partial/false matches
- [ ] Test pane capture integration
- [ ] Test background monitoring
- [ ] Mock tmux client for isolation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:02:54.929888923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:57:04.608090689Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:57:04.608007314Z","close_reason":"Added LimitDetector tests (all patterns, multiline, capture, monitoring); go test ./internal/swarm -run LimitDetector","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1t881","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1t881","depends_on_id":"bd-3un3t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1t8p","title":"EPIC: Local LLM Support via Ollama and llama.cpp","description":"## Overview
Add support for local LLM backends (Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio) as first-class agent types, enabling privacy-sensitive work, offline operation, and cost-free experimentation.

## Problem Statement
Current NTM only supports cloud LLM providers (Claude, OpenAI, Gemini). Users need:
- Privacy for sensitive codebases (air-gapped environments)
- Offline operation when internet unavailable
- Cost-free experimentation and learning
- Running specialized/fine-tuned local models

## Key Features
1. New agent type: --local=N or --ollama=N for spawning local agents
2. Model selection: --local-model=codellama:34b
3. Mixed fleets: combine cloud and local agents in same session
4. Performance monitoring: track token/s, memory usage
5. Automatic model management: pull models on demand

## Technical Design
1. Ollama adapter:
   - Connect to local Ollama server (default: localhost:11434)
   - Map NTM commands to Ollama API
   - Handle streaming responses

2. llama.cpp adapter:
   - Direct integration for llama.cpp server mode
   - Support GGUF model loading
   - GPU acceleration detection

3. Agent type hierarchy:
   - CloudAgent (existing): Claude, Codex, Gemini
   - LocalAgent (new): Ollama, LlamaCpp
   - All agents implement same interface

## Configuration
- NTM_OLLAMA_HOST for custom Ollama URL
- NTM_LOCAL_MODEL for default model
- Per-project config in ntm.yaml

## Success Criteria
- Can spawn local agents alongside cloud agents
- Local agents participate in normal workflows
- Performance metrics visible in dashboard
- Works fully offline (after model cached)

## Files Likely Affected
- internal/agent/local.go (new - local agent implementation)
- internal/agent/ollama.go (new - Ollama adapter)
- internal/cli/spawn.go (add --local flags)
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/agents.go (show local agent metrics)","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:29:25.999766320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:48:22.570133773Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1tb8","title":"Task: Add automatic model management for local LLMs","description":"## Overview
Enable NTM to automatically pull and manage local LLM models when needed.

## Deliverables
1. Model management commands:
   - ntm models list - show available local models
   - ntm models pull <model> - download model from Ollama registry
   - ntm models remove <model> - delete model to free space
   - ntm models recommend - suggest models based on hardware

2. Automatic pull on spawn:
   - If --local-model not found locally, offer to pull
   - Show download progress in terminal
   - Continue spawn after model ready

3. Model recommendations:
   Based on available VRAM:
   - <4GB: codellama:7b-instruct, deepseek-coder:6.7b
   - 4-8GB: codellama:13b, deepseek-coder:6.7b-instruct
   - 8-16GB: codellama:34b, deepseek-coder:33b
   - 16GB+: mixtral:8x7b, codellama:70b

4. Storage management:
   - Show model sizes and disk usage
   - Warn when disk space low
   - LRU cleanup suggestion for unused models

## Implementation Notes
- Use Ollama API for all model operations
- Cache model metadata locally for fast lookup
- Support offline operation with cached models

## Acceptance Criteria
- Model commands work correctly
- Automatic pull flows smoothly
- Recommendations match hardware
- Disk space warnings appear appropriately
- Unit tests for model management","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:30:23.066483753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T18:30:45.145324634Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tb8","depends_on_id":"bd-1t8p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tb8","depends_on_id":"bd-zcft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1tmw5","title":"CAAM: Add --robot-switch-account command","description":"Add robot mode command to manually trigger account switch.

## Command Specification

```bash
ntm --robot-switch-account claude         # Switch to next Claude account
ntm --robot-switch-account openai account3  # Switch to specific account
ntm --robot-switch-account --pane agent-1 claude  # Switch for specific pane
```

## Output Schema (JSON)

```json
{
  "switch": {
    "success": true,
    "provider": "claude",
    "previous_account": "account1@email.com",
    "new_account": "account2@email.com",
    "panes_affected": ["agent-1", "agent-2"],
    "cooldown_seconds": 300
  }
}
```

## Implementation
1. Parse provider and optional account name
2. Call CAAM switch
3. Update affected pane environments
4. Return result JSON

## Code Location
- internal/robot/switch_account.go
- Update: internal/robot/registry.go

## Testing
- Switch to next account, verify output
- Switch to specific account
- Switch with pane filter
- Switch when no accounts available","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:52:03.353964001Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T08:22:05.289374984Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T08:22:05.289301996Z","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-switch-account command for CAAM account switching. Added PrintSwitchAccount in robot package, flag registration in cli/root.go, and unit tests. Supports switching by provider or provider:account with optional pane filter.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-10qa5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-1vbqd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-1yty1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-26185","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-8o977","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

- Log spawn phase completion
- Log assign invocation with parameters
- Log combined result

 {"id":"bd-1tv6","title":"Task: Implement assignment algorithm","description":"## Purpose
Match beads to agents using capability scores and availability.

## Algorithm
func assignTasks(beads []Bead, agents []Agent, strategy string) []Assignment

For each bead:
1. Filter agents by availability (idle, sufficient context)
2. Score each agent: capability_score * (1 - context_usage)
3. Apply strategy modifiers:
   - balanced: spread work evenly
   - speed: assign to any available
   - quality: assign to highest scorer
   - dependency: prioritize blockers

## Assignment Struct
type Assignment struct {
    Bead       Bead
    Agent      Agent
    Score      float64
    Reason     string
    Confidence float64
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Optimal assignments based on strategy
- Handles more beads than agents
- Handles more agents than beads
- Provides reasoning for each assignment

## Technical Notes
Consider Hungarian algorithm for optimal matching
Start with greedy approach for simplicity","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:43:21.840916046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T16:26:37.782032302Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T16:26:37.781936402Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tv6","depends_on_id":"bd-9tbv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-29dnz","title":"Bug: PaneStreamer.Start leaves running=true on init error","description":"When PaneStreamer.Start() fails early (e.g., FIFO dir create fails), it returns an error but leaves ps.running=true and a cancel context allocated. This prevents retrying Start() and can leak resources. Fix: validate ctx, defer rollback of running flag + cancel on error.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:41:45.551668277Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T18:45:54.295402127Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T18:45:54.295336324Z","close_reason":"Fix PaneStreamer.Start to rollback running/cancel state on early errors; add regression test","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29dnz","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2wn","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-29kr","title":"Add session profiles - save spawn config + prompts as named profile for quick reuse","description":"Add session profiles - save spawn config + prompts as named profile for quick reuse.

## Problem Being Solved

Users frequently spawn sessions with same configuration. Profiles allow saving and reusing spawn configs.

## Command Design

### Save Profile
```bash
ntm profile save PROFILE_NAME [--from SESSION]
ntm profile save myproject --cc=3 --cod=2 --prompt-file init.md
```

### Use Profile
```bash
ntm spawn SESSION --profile PROFILE_NAME
ntm spawn daily_work --profile myproject
```

### List Profiles
```bash
ntm profile list
```

### Delete Profile
```bash
ntm profile delete PROFILE_NAME
```

## Profile Storage
~/.config/ntm/profiles/PROFILE_NAME.toml

```toml
[spawn]
cc = 3
cod = 2
gmi = 0

[prompts]
init_prompt_file = "init.md"

[options]
auto_accept = true
```

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-profile-list
ntm --robot-profile-show=PROFILE_NAME

## Acceptance Criteria

1. ntm profile save creates profile file in ~/.config/ntm/profiles/
2. ntm profile list shows all saved profiles
3. ntm profile delete removes profile
4. ntm spawn --profile loads profile and uses config
5. Profile config overridden by explicit flags
6. Robot mode returns JSON for list/show
7. Profile file uses TOML format

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/cmd/profile_test.go
- Test save/load/delete operations
- Test profile merging with flags

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-PROFILES] Session Profiles Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-PROFILES]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing session profiles"
ntm profile save test_profile --cc=2 --cod=1
ntm profile list | grep test_profile || exit 1
ntm profile delete test_profile
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Profiles test complete"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Session profiles save spawn config
2. Profiles include agent counts and prompts
3. Named profiles stored in config directory
4. ntm spawn --profile NAME loads profile
5. ntm profile list shows available profiles
6. ntm profile save NAME creates profile
7. Unit tests for profile serialization
8. E2E test for profile creation and loading","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-29nc","title":"Feature: Manual rebalance suggestions","description":"## Overview
Provide a **manual** rebalance command that analyzes workload distribution and suggests reassignments when agents leave/join or workloads become uneven, without automatic reassignment.

## User Story
As a user managing a fleet of agents, I want to see workload imbalances and get suggestions for redistributing work so that all agents are utilized efficiently and no single agent becomes a bottleneck.

## When to Rebalance
- An agent dies unexpectedly (reassign its work)
- An agent is spawned (can take on work from overloaded agents)
- Workloads become uneven (some idle, some overwhelmed)
- Priority changes (high-priority work should go to available agents)

## Scope
- `ntm rebalance <session>` analyzes and produces recommended reassignment map
- Uses assignment tracking + agent status to propose moves
- `--apply` prompts for confirmation before reassigning
- `--format json` for robot mode integration
- `--dry-run` shows what would change without prompting

## Workload Metrics
```
Current Workload Distribution:
  cc_1:  ████████████████████ 5 tasks (overloaded)
  cc_2:  ████████             2 tasks
  cc_3:  ████                 1 task
  cod_1: ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ 0 tasks (idle)
  cod_2: DEAD                 -- (unavailable)

Imbalance Score: 0.73 (high - rebalance recommended)
```

## Rebalance Algorithm
1. **Identify sources** - Agents with above-average workload
2. **Identify targets** - Agents with below-average workload or idle
3. **Calculate transfers** - Move tasks from sources to targets
4. **Respect constraints**:
   - Don't move tasks that are in-progress
   - Prefer moving tasks to agents of same type (cc→cc)
   - Respect task affinity (some tasks may be locked to specific agents)
   - Consider priority (move lower priority tasks first)

## Suggestion Output
```
Rebalance Suggestions for 'myproject':

Recommended Transfers:
  1. [bd-123] "Fix auth bug"
     cc_1 → cod_1
     Reason: cc_1 overloaded (5 tasks), cod_1 idle

  2. [bd-456] "Write tests"
     cc_1 → cc_3
     Reason: Even out Claude agent workload

  3. [bd-789] "Update docs"
     cc_2 → cod_1
     Reason: Low priority task, cod_1 available

After rebalance:
  cc_1:  ████████████         3 tasks
  cc_2:  ████                 1 task
  cc_3:  ████████             2 tasks
  cod_1: ████████             2 tasks

Apply these changes? (y/n/edit)
```

## Robot Mode Output
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "imbalance_score": 0.73,
  "transfers": [
    {
      "task_id": "bd-123",
      "task_title": "Fix auth bug",
      "from_agent": "cc_1",
      "to_agent": "cod_1",
      "reason": "source_overloaded"
    }
  ],
  "before": {"cc_1": 5, "cc_2": 2, "cc_3": 1, "cod_1": 0},
  "after": {"cc_1": 3, "cc_2": 1, "cc_3": 2, "cod_1": 2}
}
```

## CLI Examples
```bash
# Show rebalance suggestions (no action)
ntm rebalance myproject

# Show suggestions and apply after confirmation
ntm rebalance myproject --apply

# Dry run - show what would happen
ntm rebalance myproject --dry-run

# Robot mode for scripting
ntm rebalance myproject --format json

# Only rebalance Claude agents
ntm rebalance myproject --filter cc

# Set imbalance threshold (only suggest if score above threshold)
ntm rebalance myproject --threshold 0.5
```

## Handling Dead Agents
When an agent dies:
```
Agent cod_2 detected as DEAD (no response for 5m)

Orphaned tasks: 3
  - [bd-111] "Implement feature X" (in_progress)
  - [bd-222] "Review PR #42" (pending)
  - [bd-333] "Update config" (pending)

Reassignment suggestions:
  1. [bd-111] → cc_1 (similar work in progress)
  2. [bd-222] → cc_2 (available)
  3. [bd-333] → cod_1 (same agent type)

Apply? (y/n)
```

## Non‑Goals
- No automatic/background rebalancing
- No AI-based task matching (simple heuristics only)
- No cross-session rebalancing

## Implementation Notes
- Requires assignment tracking (bd-92e1) to know current assignments
- Uses agent status from tmux to detect dead/idle agents
- Imbalance score: stddev(workloads) / mean(workloads)
- Rebalance is advisory - user always confirms

## Tests
- Unit: imbalance score calculation
- Unit: transfer suggestion algorithm
- Unit: constraint handling (in-progress, affinity)
- E2E: simulate agent death → rebalance suggestions
- E2E: simulate new agent spawn → rebalance suggestions
- E2E: apply rebalance with confirmation

## Logging
```go
log.Printf("[E2E-REBALANCE] session=%s imbalance=%.2f suggestions=%d", session, score, count)
log.Printf("[E2E-REBALANCE] transfer task=%s from=%s to=%s reason=%s", task, from, to, reason)
log.Printf("[E2E-REBALANCE] applied=%t transfers=%d errors=%d", applied, transfers, errors)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Recommendations deterministic for same state
- [ ] Safe confirmation before applying
- [ ] Works even if some agents unavailable
- [ ] Detects dead agents and suggests reassignment
- [ ] Respects in-progress task constraint
- [ ] Imbalance score calculated correctly
- [ ] Robot mode outputs valid JSON
- [ ] Dry-run shows changes without prompting

## Dependencies
- Requires bd-92e1 (assignment tracking) to function","notes":"Rescoped from automatic rebalancing to manual suggestions to avoid hidden state changes.\n\n## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-2a5yf","title":"Add ntm assign --clear for removing assignment from agent","description":"## Overview
Allow users to clear/cancel an assignment, returning the bead to the unassigned pool.

## Use Cases
1. Agent crashed and assignment is stale
2. User wants to restart a bead from scratch
3. Bead was assigned by mistake
4. Agent gave up and user wants to retry later

## Usage
```bash
# Clear single assignment
ntm assign myproject --clear bd-xyz

# Clear multiple assignments
ntm assign myproject --clear bd-xyz,bd-abc,bd-def

# Clear all assignments for a pane (agent crashed)
ntm assign myproject --clear-pane=3

# Clear all failed assignments
ntm assign myproject --clear-failed
```

## Workflow
1. Validate bead is currently assigned
2. Update assignment status to "cleared"
3. Release file reservations (call Agent Mail release_file_reservations)
4. Bead is now available for reassignment
5. Optionally update bead status in br (if bead was marked in-progress)

## Output (Standard Envelope)
```json
{
  "command": "assign",
  "subcommand": "clear",
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "cleared": [
      {
        "bead_id": "bd-xyz",
        "bead_title": "Implement feature X",
        "previous_pane": 3,
        "previous_agent": "GreenCastle",
        "previous_status": "working",
        "file_reservations_released": true
      }
    ],
    "summary": {
      "cleared_count": 1,
      "reservations_released": 1
    }
  },
  "warnings": [],
  "error": null
}
```

## Error Output
```json
{
  "command": "assign",
  "subcommand": "clear",
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
  "success": false,
  "data": null,
  "warnings": [],
  "error": {
    "code": "NOT_ASSIGNED",
    "message": "bead bd-xyz is not currently assigned"
  }
}
```

## Error Codes
- NOT_ASSIGNED: Bead doesn't have an active assignment
- ALREADY_COMPLETED: Bead assignment already completed (use --force)
- PANE_NOT_FOUND: Specified pane doesn't exist

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Clear single assignment
- [ ] Clear multiple assignments (comma-separated)
- [ ] Clear by pane (--clear-pane)
- [ ] Clear failed assignments (--clear-failed)
- [ ] Release file reservations via Agent Mail
- [ ] Standard JSON envelope output
- [ ] --force flag for completed beads
- [ ] Clear error codes

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Assignment clearing logic
- Unit test: File reservation release
- Unit test: Output format validation
- E2E test: Clear and re-assign workflow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:37:03.312294007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:41:18.368144333Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:41:18.368099689Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2a5yf","depends_on_id":"bd-1ohy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2a5yf","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2a5yf","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2qfa5","title":"Task: Define ReasoningMode struct and related types in internal/ensemble/types.go","description":"# Task: Define ReasoningMode struct and related types

**STATUS: PARTIALLY IMPLEMENTED**

The following types are now implemented in internal/ensemble/types.go:

## Implemented Types

### Core Mode Types
- ReasoningMode - Mode definition with ID, Name, Category, etc.
- ReasoningCategory - Category enum (Formal, Ampliative, etc.)
- ModeCatalog - Indexed catalog with lookup methods

### Output Schema Types (NEW - Critical for mechanical merging)
- ImpactLevel - high/medium/low enum with validation
- Confidence - 0.0-1.0 score with validation
- Finding - Discovery with impact, confidence, evidence_pointer
- Risk - Potential problem with severity, likelihood
- Recommendation - Suggested action with priority, effort
- Question - Unresolved query for user
- FailureModeWarning - Reasoning pitfall warning
- ModeOutput - Complete output schema (mandatory for all modes)

### Configuration Types
- BudgetConfig - Token limits, timeouts, retries
- SynthesisConfig - Strategy, min confidence, max findings
- Ensemble - Curated mode collection (primary user interface)
- SynthesisStrategy - Enum (consensus, adversarial, etc.)

## Remaining Work
- None - types are complete

## Test Coverage
- types_test.go includes comprehensive validation tests
- All Validate() methods tested
- Default config functions tested

 {"id":"bd-2qwm8","title":"Feature: Synthesis System - Combining multi-mode outputs","description":"# Feature: Synthesis System - Stage 3 of pipeline

**Two-path synthesis architecture:**
- **Agent-based**: Synthesizer + Disagreement Auditor
- **Manual/Mechanical**: No synthesizer agent; deterministic merge + auditor only

## Stage 3 Components

### 1. Output Collector
- Validate each output against OutputSchema
- Flag non-compliant outputs
- Aggregate into SynthesisInput

### 2. Synthesizer Agent (when strategy requires agent)
- Merge outputs into cohesive report
- Rank findings by impact × confidence
- Generate executive summary

### 3. Disagreement Auditor
- Identify contradictory theses and risks
- Surface conflicts and resolution paths

### 4. Output Formatter
- Markdown/JSON/YAML export of SynthesisResult
- Deterministic ordering

## Strategy Support
- Manual strategies should **skip synthesizer agent** but still run auditor + formatter.
- Agent strategies use templates from bd-3k4sh and definitions from bd-2ztw9.

## Files
- internal/ensemble/collector.go
- internal/ensemble/synthesizer.go
- internal/ensemble/auditor.go
- internal/ensemble/merge.go
- internal/ensemble/formatter.go","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"claude-opus-4.5","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:26:02.694077251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:56:29.175540344Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T07:56:29.175514615Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qwm8","depends_on_id":"bd-12u4u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qwm8","depends_on_id":"bd-2ouwm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qwm8","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2rf60","title":"Task: Implement handoff reader module","description":"## Overview
Implement the handoff reader module that finds and parses the most recent handoff for a session with caching for performance.

## Background & Reasoning
The reader is critical for:
1. Session-start context restoration - must be fast
2. Status line goal/now extraction - called frequently
3. Resume command functionality - needs full handoff
4. Dashboard handoff display - periodic refresh

Performance matters: status line refreshes every few seconds, so we cache and invalidate smartly.

## Deliverables

### 1. Create internal/handoff/reader.go
```go
package handoff

import (
    "log/slog"
    "os"
    "path/filepath"
    "regexp"
    "sort"
    "strings"
    "sync"
    "time"

    "gopkg.in/yaml.v3"
)

var (
    goalRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^goal:\s*(.+)$`)
    nowRegex  = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^now:\s*(.+)$`)
)

// Reader handles reading handoff files from disk with caching
type Reader struct {
    baseDir string
    logger  *slog.Logger

    // Cache for ExtractGoalNow (most frequent operation)
    cacheMu       sync.RWMutex
    goalNowCache  map[string]goalNowEntry
    cacheExpiry   time.Duration
}

type goalNowEntry struct {
    goal      string
    now       string
    fetchedAt time.Time
    modTime   time.Time // file mod time when fetched
}

// NewReader creates a Reader for the given project directory
func NewReader(projectDir string) *Reader {
    return &Reader{
        baseDir:      filepath.Join(projectDir, ".ntm", "handoffs"),
        logger:       slog.Default().With("component", "handoff.reader"),
        goalNowCache: make(map[string]goalNowEntry),
        cacheExpiry:  30 * time.Second,
    }
}

// FindLatest returns the most recent handoff for a session
// Returns (nil, "", nil) if no handoffs exist (not an error)
func (r *Reader) FindLatest(sessionName string) (*Handoff, string, error) {
    r.logger.Debug("finding latest handoff", "session", sessionName)

    dir := filepath.Join(r.baseDir, sessionName)

    entries, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
    if os.IsNotExist(err) {
        r.logger.Debug("no handoff directory", "session", sessionName)
        return nil, "", nil // No directory = no handoffs, not an error
    }
    if err != nil {
        r.logger.Error("failed to read handoff directory",
            "dir", dir,
            "error", err,
        )
        return nil, "", fmt.Errorf("readdir failed: %w", err)
    }

    // Find YAML files (exclude .archive directory)
    var yamlFiles []os.DirEntry
    for _, e := range entries {
        if !e.IsDir() && strings.HasSuffix(e.Name(), ".yaml") {
            yamlFiles = append(yamlFiles, e)
        }
    }

    if len(yamlFiles) == 0 {
        r.logger.Debug("no handoff files found", "session", sessionName)
        return nil, "", nil
    }

    // Sort by name descending (timestamps in filename = chronological)
    sort.Slice(yamlFiles, func(i, j int) bool {
        return yamlFiles[i].Name() > yamlFiles[j].Name()
    })

    // Read most recent
    path := filepath.Join(dir, yamlFiles[0].Name())
    h, err := r.Read(path)
    if err != nil {
        r.logger.Error("failed to read latest handoff",
            "path", path,
            "error", err,
        )
        return nil, "", err
    }

    r.logger.Debug("found latest handoff",
        "session", sessionName,
        "path", path,
        "goal", truncateLog(h.Goal, 30),
    )

    return h, path, nil
}

// FindLatestAny returns the most recent handoff across all sessions
func (r *Reader) FindLatestAny() (*Handoff, string, error) {
    r.logger.Debug("finding latest handoff across all sessions")

    entries, err := os.ReadDir(r.baseDir)
    if os.IsNotExist(err) {
        r.logger.Debug("no handoffs directory exists")
        return nil, "", nil
    }
    if err != nil {
        return nil, "", fmt.Errorf("readdir base failed: %w", err)
    }

    var newest *Handoff
    var newestPath string
    var newestTime time.Time

    for _, e := range entries {
        if !e.IsDir() {
            continue
        }
        h, path, err := r.FindLatest(e.Name())
        if err != nil || h == nil {
            continue
        }
        if h.CreatedAt.After(newestTime) {
            newest = h
            newestPath = path
            newestTime = h.CreatedAt
        }
    }

    if newest != nil {
        r.logger.Debug("found latest handoff across all",
            "path", newestPath,
            "session", newest.Session,
        )
    }

    return newest, newestPath, nil
}

// Read parses a specific handoff file
func (r *Reader) Read(path string) (*Handoff, error) {
    r.logger.Debug("reading handoff", "path", path)

    data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
    if err != nil {
        r.logger.Error("failed to read handoff file",
            "path", path,
            "error", err,
        )
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("read failed: %w", err)
    }

    var h Handoff
    if err := yaml.Unmarshal(data, &h); err != nil {
        r.logger.Error("failed to parse handoff YAML",
            "path", path,
            "error", err,
            "size", len(data),
        )
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("unmarshal failed: %w", err)
    }

    // Validate on read
    if errs := h.Validate(); len(errs) > 0 {
        r.logger.Warn("handoff has validation issues",
            "path", path,
            "error_count", len(errs),
            "first_error", errs[0].Error(),
        )
        // Continue anyway - allow reading partial/old handoffs
    }

    r.logger.Debug("handoff read successfully",
        "path", path,
        "session", h.Session,
        "has_goal", h.Goal != "",
        "has_now", h.Now != "",
    )

    return &h, nil
}

// ExtractGoalNow extracts just goal and now fields efficiently
// For status line use - uses regex and caching for speed
func (r *Reader) ExtractGoalNow(sessionName string) (goal, now string, err error) {
    // Find latest file path first
    dir := filepath.Join(r.baseDir, sessionName)
    entries, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
    if err != nil {
        if os.IsNotExist(err) {
            return "", "", nil
        }
        return "", "", err
    }

    // Find most recent YAML
    var latest string
    for _, e := range entries {
        if !e.IsDir() && strings.HasSuffix(e.Name(), ".yaml") {
            if e.Name() > latest {
                latest = e.Name()
            }
        }
    }
    if latest == "" {
        return "", "", nil
    }

    path := filepath.Join(dir, latest)

    // Check cache
    r.cacheMu.RLock()
    entry, ok := r.goalNowCache[path]
    r.cacheMu.RUnlock()

    if ok {
        // Check if cache is still valid
        info, err := os.Stat(path)
        if err == nil && info.ModTime() == entry.modTime && time.Since(entry.fetchedAt) < r.cacheExpiry {
            r.logger.Debug("cache hit for goal/now",
                "path", path,
                "age_ms", time.Since(entry.fetchedAt).Milliseconds(),
            )
            return entry.goal, entry.now, nil
        }
    }

    // Cache miss or expired - read file
    r.logger.Debug("cache miss for goal/now, reading file", "path", path)

    content, err := os.ReadFile(path)
    if err != nil {
        return "", "", err
    }

    // Fast regex extraction
    if match := goalRegex.FindSubmatch(content); match != nil {
        goal = strings.TrimSpace(string(match[1]))
    }
    if match := nowRegex.FindSubmatch(content); match != nil {
        now = strings.TrimSpace(string(match[1]))
    }

    // Update cache
    info, _ := os.Stat(path)
    r.cacheMu.Lock()
    r.goalNowCache[path] = goalNowEntry{
        goal:      goal,
        now:       now,
        fetchedAt: time.Now(),
        modTime:   info.ModTime(),
    }
    r.cacheMu.Unlock()

    r.logger.Debug("extracted goal/now",
        "path", path,
        "goal_len", len(goal),
        "now_len", len(now),
    )

    return goal, now, nil
}

// InvalidateCache clears the goal/now cache
// Call when you know handoffs have been written
func (r *Reader) InvalidateCache() {
    r.cacheMu.Lock()
    r.goalNowCache = make(map[string]goalNowEntry)
    r.cacheMu.Unlock()
    r.logger.Debug("cache invalidated")
}

// ListHandoffs returns all handoffs for a session, sorted by date desc
func (r *Reader) ListHandoffs(sessionName string) ([]HandoffMeta, error) {
    r.logger.Debug("listing handoffs", "session", sessionName)

    dir := filepath.Join(r.baseDir, sessionName)
    entries, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
    if os.IsNotExist(err) {
        return nil, nil
    }
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("readdir failed: %w", err)
    }

    var metas []HandoffMeta
    for _, e := range entries {
        if e.IsDir() || !strings.HasSuffix(e.Name(), ".yaml") {
            continue
        }

        path := filepath.Join(dir, e.Name())
        info, _ := e.Info()

        // Quick extract goal/now without full parse
        goal, _, _ := r.extractGoalNowDirect(path)

        meta := HandoffMeta{
            Path:    path,
            Session: sessionName,
            Goal:    goal,
            IsAuto:  strings.HasPrefix(e.Name(), "auto-handoff-"),
        }

        if info != nil {
            meta.Date = info.ModTime()
        }

        // Extract status from file (quick regex)
        if content, err := os.ReadFile(path); err == nil {
            if match := regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^status:\s*(\w+)`).FindSubmatch(content); match != nil {
                meta.Status = string(match[1])
            }
        }

        metas = append(metas, meta)
    }

    // Sort by date descending
    sort.Slice(metas, func(i, j int) bool {
        return metas[i].Date.After(metas[j].Date)
    })

    r.logger.Debug("listed handoffs",
        "session", sessionName,
        "count", len(metas),
    )

    return metas, nil
}

// extractGoalNowDirect does regex extraction without caching
func (r *Reader) extractGoalNowDirect(path string) (goal, now string, err error) {
    content, err := os.ReadFile(path)
    if err != nil {
        return "", "", err
    }
    if match := goalRegex.FindSubmatch(content); match != nil {
        goal = strings.TrimSpace(string(match[1]))
    }
    if match := nowRegex.FindSubmatch(content); match != nil {
        now = strings.TrimSpace(string(match[1]))
    }
    return goal, now, nil
}

type HandoffMeta struct {
    Path    string
    Session string
    Date    time.Time
    Status  string
    Goal    string // For quick display
    IsAuto  bool
}

func truncateLog(s string, max int) string {
    if len(s) <= max {
        return s
    }
    return s[:max] + "..."
}
```

### 2. File Discovery Logic
1. List all .yaml files in `.ntm/handoffs/{session}/`
2. Exclude `.archive/` directory
3. Sort by filename descending (timestamps in name = chronological)
4. Return most recent

### 3. Cache Strategy
- Cache goal/now extractions (most frequent operation for status line)
- Cache key: file path
- Cache invalidation triggers:
  1. File mod time changed
  2. Cache entry older than 30 seconds
  3. Explicit InvalidateCache() call after writes
- Thread-safe with sync.RWMutex

### 4. Error Handling Strategy
- Directory not found → return nil, not error (no handoffs is valid state)
- File read error → return error with context
- YAML parse error → return error with size info for debugging
- Validation errors → log warning but return handoff (allow partial reads)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FindLatest returns most recent handoff for session
- [ ] FindLatest returns (nil, "", nil) when no handoffs exist
- [ ] FindLatestAny searches across all sessions
- [ ] Read parses full YAML into Handoff struct
- [ ] Read logs validation warnings but doesn't fail
- [ ] ExtractGoalNow uses regex for speed
- [ ] ExtractGoalNow caches results with mod time tracking
- [ ] Cache expires after 30 seconds
- [ ] InvalidateCache clears the cache
- [ ] ListHandoffs returns sorted HandoffMeta slice
- [ ] All operations skip .archive directory
- [ ] All operations logged with structured slog
- [ ] Handles missing directories gracefully
- [ ] Handles malformed YAML with clear error messages
- [ ] Unit tests for all reader methods
- [ ] Unit tests for cache hit/miss/invalidation
- [ ] Unit tests for malformed file handling

## Technical Notes
- Use gopkg.in/yaml.v3 for YAML parsing
- Use regexp with (?m) multiline flag for goal/now extraction
- Cache file mod time to detect changes without re-reading
- Thread-safe caching is critical - status line may call from different goroutines
- Log at DEBUG level for normal operations, WARN for recoverable issues, ERROR for failures

## Logging Requirements
- DEBUG: FindLatest/FindLatestAny start and result
- DEBUG: Read operation with path
- DEBUG: Cache hit with age
- DEBUG: Cache miss, reading file
- DEBUG: Cache invalidation
- DEBUG: ListHandoffs with count
- WARN: Validation issues on read (with error count)
- ERROR: Directory read failures
- ERROR: File read failures
